signature: Lindley recursion W_{n+1}=max(W_n+X_n,0) and one-sided absorbed/reflected walks whose increments are driven by a DETERMINISTIC symbolic itinerary (or weakly-dependent/Markov approximation of one): worst-case and persistence behavior of (max,+)-linear functionals under language- or discrepancy-constrained input words. Cryptid instantiation: Antihydra halts iff the +2/-1 walk driven by the parity word of the floor(3n/2)-orbit hits -1, i.e. iff the Lindley sup-functional of the reversed word crosses an exact boundary against positive drift.

finds.ref: C. Bandi, D. Bertsimas, N. Youssef, "Robust Queueing Theory", Operations Research 63(3), 2015
finds.the_theorem: Assumption 1: interarrivals lie in the polyhedral uncertainty set U^a = {T : sum_{i=k+1}^n T_i - (n-k)/lambda >= -Gamma_a (n-k)^{1/alpha_a} for ALL 0<=k<=n-1} (a one-sided CLT/stable-law-shaped partial-sum constraint; deterministic, no probability), services dually in U^s. Eq.(12) [verified from PDF]: the worst-case Lindley system time is EXACTLY S_n = max_{1<=k<=n} {(n-k+1)/mu + Gamma_s(n-k+1)^{1/alpha_s} - (n-k)/lambda + Gamma_a(n-k)^{1/alpha_a}}, achieved by an explicit extremal sample path (Prop. 1). Theorem 2: for alpha_a=alpha_s=alpha, rho=lambda/mu<1, S_n <= [(alpha-1)/alpha^{alpha/(alpha-1)}] * lambda^{1/(alpha-1)}(Gamma_a+Gamma_s)^{alpha/(alpha-1)}/(1-rho)^{1/(alpha-1)} + 1/lambda, uniformly in n.
finds.the_match: Antihydra's counter c_n = c_0 + 2E_n - O_n (E/O = even/odd counts) halts iff c hits -1, iff the Lindley sup-functional max_k of suffix sums of the negated step word crosses a fixed boundary — literally BBY's Eq.(2) with 'service totals' = odd-step contributions and 'arrival totals' = even-step contributions. Since E_n+O_n=n, halting at n forces O_n=(c_0+2n+1)/3 ~ 2n/3, a LINEAR one-sided excess over the drift density 1/2. If the parity word satisfies the U^a-type certificate #odd(k..n] <= (n-k)/2 + Gamma(n-k)^{1/alpha} on all suffix windows, Eq.(12) yields an exact finite drawdown bound, hence non-halting beyond explicit n_0(Gamma,alpha). GAPS, flagged: (a) proving the certificate for the one specific orbit from n=8 is the open core — the theorem converts the problem, it does not solve it; (b) BBY optimize system time, not exact-boundary hitting, but hitting -1 implies drawdown >= threshold so the bound applies verbatim; (c) their motivation is probabilistic queues, ours is a single deterministic orbit — the math is deterministic optimization and transfers exactly.
finds.transfer: Prove: 'If the Antihydra parity word lies in U(Gamma,alpha) then the machine never halts after an explicit step n_0(Gamma,alpha); equivalently halting at time n forces a one-sided windowed parity discrepancy >= explicit linear function of n, with the exact threshold given by Eq.(12).' Then average-case: Terras-style level-k equidistribution puts almost-all starting values in U(Gamma,alpha) up to horizon f(k), giving density-1 non-halting-window statements for the Hydra family.

finds.ref: D.A. van der Laan, "The structure and performance of optimal routing sequences", PhD thesis, Leiden University, 2003 (chapters with A. Hordijk; companion: E. Altman, B. Gaujal, A. Hordijk, "Balanced sequences and optimal routing", J. ACM 47(4), 2000, and "Multimodularity, convexity and optimization properties", Math. OR 25, 2000)
finds.the_theorem: Defines a combinatorial 'unbalance' I(u)>=0 of a periodic 0-1 word u (I(u)=0 iff u is regular = balanced/Sturmian/Beatty), plus a graph partial order. Theorem 3.3.8 [verified]: PURE SAMPLE-PATH deterministic comparison — if u graph-below v with density k/T and interarrival Cesaro limsups <= delta, then (1/m)(sum_j W_v(j) - sum_j W_u(j)) <= delta*(T/k)*(I(v)-I(u)) for the single-server Lindley waiting times. Theorem 3.3.11/Corollaries 3.3.12-14: with i.i.d. drivers, rho<1: W(v)-W(u) <= (delta/d)(I(v)-I(u)), hence W(u) <= W(omega(d)) + (delta/d) I(u) where omega(d) is the regular word of the same density, and a dual lower bound via dual unbalance. AGH 2000: for any sequence of multimodular cost functions (Lindley/workload functionals of (max,+)-linear systems are proven multimodular), long-run average cost over binary words of fixed density is minimized by the regular (Sturmian) word.
finds.the_match: This is the closest existing thing to the hunted 'comparison lemma bounding deterministic-driver Lindley functionals by an idealized driver plus a quantitative equidistribution penalty': performance(word) - performance(balanced word of same density) is sandwiched by unbalance, which is exactly a discrepancy functional of the parity word. Cryptid counter = workload functional of the parity word. GAPS, flagged: (a) theorems control long-run AVERAGE waiting time, not first-passage/boundary-hitting — a persistence analog must be proved; (b) unbalance and the clean theorems are for periodic words (finite-word variants exist in Sec. 3.2 but the sharp statements are periodic); (c) graph order is partial, not total — not every pair of words comparable; (d) balanced words have bounded remainder, so a balanced density-1/2 driver keeps the counter within O(1) of the drift line and never hits -1 — the theorems quantify how far from balanced the parity word must be for halting, but say nothing about whether the actual orbit word is that unbalanced.
finds.transfer: Prove the first-passage analog: min-drawdown(u_{[0,n]}) >= drawdown(omega_{1/2}) - c*I(u_{[0,n]}) for the counter functional; conclude 'Antihydra halts at n implies I(parity prefix) >= explicit linear function of n'. Separately, use the multimodular-order machinery to characterize the EXTREMAL words of given density for the drawdown functional (most-unbalanced words), giving the exact halting-boundary threshold as a function of density and unbalance — the deterministic-driver replacement for the (1/phi)^n calculation.

finds.ref: S. Gaubert, "Performance evaluation of (max,+) automata", IEEE Trans. Automatic Control 40(12), 1995
finds.the_theorem: A (max,+) automaton (alpha, mu, beta) assigns to each word w the max path weight (y|w)=alpha mu(w) beta. Theorem 2 (Worst Case Evaluation) [verified]: if M = oplus_{a in Sigma} mu(a) is irreducible with cyclicity c, then for k large (y|Sigma^{k+c}) = rho(M)^c tensor (y|Sigma^k), i.e. the worst case over all words of length k grows linearly with rate rho(M) = max cycle mean (tropical spectral radius, Karp-computable); if the representation is trim, rho(M) = limsup_k (y|Sigma^k)^{1/k} without irreducibility. Section VI, Proposition 1 [verified]: worst case CONSTRAINED TO A REGULAR SUBLANGUAGE L equals the same formula for the tensor product M'' = oplus_a mu(a) tensor^t mu'(a) with the boolean automaton of L: (y|L cap Sigma^{k+c}) = rho(M'')^c (y|L cap Sigma^k) when M'' irreducible.
finds.the_match: The counter max-drawdown functional max_{k<=n}(-(c_k - c_0)) is recognized by a 2-state (max,+) automaton over {even,odd} with weights (-2,+1) — so 'does any admissible parity word drive the counter to the boundary at linear rate' is exactly Gaubert's language-constrained worst-case problem, solved by a tropical eigenvalue of a product automaton. GAPS, flagged: (a) for Collatz-type maps the Terras bijection makes every finite parity word occur as a prefix of SOME orbit, so the full-language worst case is trivial; nontrivial certificates need regular over-approximations of the actual orbit language (e.g. from residue-class avoidance), and density constraints are not regular; (b) irreducibility of the tensor product must be checked per instance; (c) single-orbit problems (Antihydra from n=8) need orbit-specific language information that no finite automaton is yet known to capture — cf. our <=7-state DFA negative result.
finds.transfer: A 'tropical path-complete certificate' pipeline: enumerate candidate regular languages L consistent with the rational transduction T (or with proven residue-avoidance facts), form the tensor product of L's DFA with the 2-state counter automaton, compute the tropical spectral radius rho(M''); rho(M'') < boundary drift rate certifies non-halting/one-sided boundedness for every driver in L. This is the exact (max,+) analog of the path-complete Lyapunov certificates already in our library (Ahmadi-Jungers-Parrilo-Roozbehani, Philippe et al.), but with tropical Perron theory replacing SOS, and it computes the certificate in polynomial time per candidate L.

finds.ref: L. Daviaud, P. Guillon, G. Merlet, "Comparison of max-plus automata and joint spectral radius of tropical matrices", MFCS 2017 (arXiv:1612.02647)
finds.the_theorem: [Verified from PDF] Theorem 1/2: the positivity problem for max-plus automata ('is there a word with value >= 0') is undecidable already for weights in {-1,1} with 2 states over Z_max, and remains undecidable when all states are initial and final. Theorem 3: deciding whether the tropical joint spectral radius rho(Gamma) >= 0 is undecidable for finite sets of matrices with entries in {-infty,-1,0,1} (3 states). Theorem 4: ultimate rank uncomputable. Theorem 5: rho(Gamma) is APPROXIMABLE — an algorithm computes alpha with alpha - 1/n <= rho(Gamma) <= alpha + 1/n for every n. Theorem 6: for matrices with FINITE entries (complete automata) the tropical JSR is a rational number computable in PSPACE, and the problem is PSPACE-complete for weights {0,-1}.
finds.the_match: Our certificate question 'does some admissible parity word keep the (max,+)-linear counter functional above the boundary' IS a positivity/tropical-JSR question. These theorems delimit the certificate landscape exactly: undecidable in general (so no universal algorithm over arbitrary weighted-automaton constraints — the same wall our DFA-invariant-set search hit), but decidable in PSPACE when the product automaton has finite entries, and always approximable to any precision. GAPS, flagged: (a) tensoring the counter automaton with a proper DFA constraint introduces -infty entries, so Theorem 6's finite-entry case does not directly apply to constrained products (Theorem 5 approximability always does); (b) undecidability is a ceiling statement about the general class, not about our specific structured instances.
finds.transfer: Implement Theorem 5's approximation algorithm (and Theorem 6's PSPACE procedure where products stay finite-entried) as the decision engine for the Gaubert pipeline in the previous find: for each candidate regular constraint L, decide 'certified drawdown rate < boundary rate' to precision 1/n. Also transfer the undecidability constructions to sharpen our meta-theorems about which certificate formats can possibly settle cryptid non-halting (analog and strengthening of our <=7-state DFA result, now for weighted/counter certificates).

finds.ref: Q. Berger, L. Bethencourt, "An application of Sparre Andersen's fluctuation theorem for exchangeable and sign-invariant random variables", 2023, arXiv:2304.09031
finds.the_theorem: [Verified from PDF] Theorem 2.1: if (xi_1..xi_n) is exchangeable AND sign-invariant (law invariant under all sign flips), then P(S_k > 0 for all k<=n) <= g(n) := 4^{-n} binom(2n,n) <= P(S_k >= 0 for all k<=n), with equality throughout when the law has no atoms — persistence is DISTRIBUTION-FREE. Theorem 3.1: for a symmetric recurrent birth-death chain X on Z and odd sign-preserving f, the additive functional zeta_n = sum f(X_i) satisfies p_0(1-p_0) E[g(L_n - 1)] <= P(zeta_k > 0, k<=n) <= E[g(L_n)], where L_n = local time of X at 0; Prop. 3.2 converts return-time tails into explicit persistence asymptotics (recovers Sinai's n^{-1/4} for integrated SRW).
finds.the_match: Cryptid counters are exactly additive functionals zeta_n = sum f(X_i) of the residue process X_i = (orbit mod 2^k), with f = counter step (+2 on even, -1 on odd) — the shape of Theorem 3.1 with the mod-2^k Terras chain in place of the birth-death chain. GAPS, serious and flagged: (a) sign-invariance FAILS outright for our drifted asymmetric (+2/-1) steps, so the universal g(n) does not transfer; (b) their chain must be symmetric and 'unable to jump over 0'; (c) our driver is deterministic — exchangeability holds only approximately at Terras level with unquantified error. What transfers is the TECHNIQUE: excursion decomposition of the driver chain + sandwich of the persistence probability between functionals of the local time at a reference state.
finds.transfer: Prove a cryptid analog of Theorem 3.1: condition on returns of the mod-2^k residue chain to a reference class, treat counter increments over excursions as i.i.d.-like blocks with explicit Terras-approximation error, and sandwich P(counter avoids -1 up to n) between local-time functionals — converting level-k equidistribution into rigorous two-sided persistence bounds for the Markov-approximated Antihydra, with error terms that are explicit in k.

finds.ref: F. Aurzada, S. Mukherjee, O. Zeitouni, "Persistence exponents in Markov chains", Ann. Inst. H. Poincare Probab. Statist. 57(3), 2021 (arXiv:1703.06447)
finds.the_theorem: [Verified from PDF] Theorem 1.2: let K be a non-negative linear operator mapping C_b(S) to itself with K^k compact for some k>=1, and mu a probability measure charging every nonempty open subset of S. Then lim_n (1/n) log integral_S K^n 1 dmu = log lambda(K), where lambda(K) is the operator spectral radius; if lambda(K)>0 it is the largest eigenvalue, with a non-negative eigenfunction in C_b(S) and a finitely additive non-negative left eigenmeasure (Krein-Rutman). Applied to the killed kernel P_S g(x) = int_S g(y) P(x,dy): the persistence exponent lim (1/n) log P_mu(X_0..X_n in S) exists and equals log of the spectral radius of the killed operator; continuity/monotonicity of the exponent in the kernel parameters is developed (Sec. 1.2, Lemma 5.3).
finds.the_match: The Markov-approximated cryptid is exactly their object: chain = (residue mod 2^k, counter value), S = {counter >= 0}, and 'not yet halted by n' is the persistence probability; the exponent is the spectral radius of an explicit killed transfer operator. For i.i.d. parities the eigenvalue equation reproduces the cryptid heuristic exactly: the down-crossing rate y solves (1/2)(y^{-2}+y)=1, i.e. y = phi, giving the (1/phi)^n halting heuristic — and the Markov-modulated version (Perron root of the level-k matrix MGF, classical Lundberg theory a la Asmussen's Markovian-environment risk model) computes the level-k corrected rate exactly. GAPS, flagged: (a) K^k-compactness fails for lattice killed walks on unbounded S (their applications smooth via continuous innovations); for our chain the exponent must instead be extracted by matrix-analytic/Wiener-Hopf methods — same conclusion, different proof; (b) mu must charge all open sets — Dirac initial data (our case: one orbit) is explicitly problematic in their framework (Prop. 2.5 discusses failure for general initial conditions); (c) nothing here touches deterministic drivers.
finds.transfer: Compute lambda_k := persistence exponent of the killed (mod 2^k)-modulated counter chain exactly, as the Perron root gamma_k of the finite matrix equation spr(F_k[gamma])=1 (level-k Lundberg exponent); prove lambda_k converges with explicit error to the i.i.d. value log phi (per unit boundary distance). Output: the rigorous 'Markov-driver side' of the desired deterministic-vs-Markov comparison, i.e. exact quantitative heuristics (1/phi)^n -> (rate_k)^n for the whole cryptid family, including Space Needle's valuation dynamics, with continuity-in-kernel (their Sec 1.2) controlling the k->infty stability.

finds.ref: J.-Y. Le Boudec, P. Thiran, "Network Calculus: A Theory of Deterministic Queuing Systems for the Internet", Springer LNCS 2050, 2001
finds.the_theorem: [Verified from PDF] Theorem 1.4.1 (Backlog Bound): if a flow with cumulative arrivals R(t) is constrained by an arrival curve alpha (R(t)-R(s) <= alpha(t-s) for all s<=t) and traverses a system offering a service curve beta, then the backlog satisfies R(t)-R*(t) <= sup_{s>=0}{alpha(s)-beta(s)} for ALL t — purely deterministic, (min,+)-algebraic, no probability. Companion tightness theorem: the bound is achieved by R=alpha, R*=min(alpha,beta). Concatenation theorem: a tandem of systems with service curves beta_1, beta_2 offers the (min,+) convolution beta_1 * beta_2 — certificates COMPOSE. Theorem 1.4.7: busy-period refinement when alpha(u_0) <= beta(u_0).
finds.the_match: Same shape as the robust-queueing find but with an algebra of composition: counter non-halting = a backlog-type deviation staying below the exact boundary, where 'arrivals' = cumulative odd-step penalty and guaranteed 'service' = even-step replenishment. A window certificate ('in any window of length s, at most alpha(s) odd steps') is precisely an arrival-curve constraint, and window certificates for transducer blocks compose via (min,+) convolution into orbit-level drawdown bounds. GAPS, flagged: (a) as with BBY, the hard part is proving a sublinear (or even (2/3-eps)-density) arrival curve for the actual orbit's parity word; (b) if the parity-vector map of floor(3n/2) is bijective mod 2^k (Terras-like — we must check), then the max over residues of window odd-counts is trivial (=k) and unconditioned window certificates are vacuous; single-orbit certificates need residue-avoidance input; (c) network calculus needs wide-sense-increasing cumulative functions — satisfied trivially here.
finds.transfer: Two-step: (1) compute m_k = max over residues mod 2^k of the odd-count over k steps of floor(3n/2); if m_k/k < 2/3 for some k, Theorem 1.4.1 + additive window composition IMMEDIATELY gives Antihydra non-halting for all n beyond an explicit n_0 — a finite computation with a chance of an unconditional result; (2) if m_k/k >= 2/3 (expected if parity map is bijective), develop conditional arrival curves alpha(s) from any provable residue-avoidance (invariant-set) facts and compose them via (min,+) convolution into quantified non-halting horizons.

best_single_bet: The robust-queueing / unbalance pair (finds 1+2, fused). It converts every cryptid halting question into an exactly quantified one-sided discrepancy certificate for the parity word — the same currency (equidistribution mod 2^k) in which all known Collatz partial results (Terras, Tao) are denominated. Week plan, first move (day 1, pure computation): write Antihydra's counter as the absorbed Lindley walk; from BBY Eq.(12) derive the exact threshold function such that halting at time n forces the parity prefix out of the uncertainty set U(Gamma,alpha) on some suffix window; simultaneously run the finite check m_k = max over residues mod 2^k of odd-counts in k steps of floor(3n/2) (find 7's transfer) — if m_k/k ever drops below 2/3 the problem is FINISHED unconditionally by window composition, and if not, the computed m_k profile plus the measured unbalance I(u) of the first 10^7 actual parity bits calibrates exactly how much equidistribution a Terras-style argument must supply. Days 2-7: prove the first-passage analog of van der Laan Theorem 3.3.8 (sample-path drawdown vs. unbalance) and package 'halting at n implies one-sided windowed discrepancy >= explicit linear function of n' as the first rigorous cryptid partial result, with the density-1 starting-value corollary via level-k equidistribution.
dead_ends: (1) The dream lemma — a rigorous coupling/comparison theorem bounding DETERMINISTIC-driver persistence by Markov-driver persistence given quantitative equidistribution mod 2^k — does NOT exist anywhere I searched (persistence literature, queueing, ruin theory, fluctuation theory). Closest brackets: van der Laan's unbalance comparison (long-run averages only, not first passage) and BBY robust queueing (worst-case over discrepancy-constrained sets). Stop searching for it; it must be proved. (2) 'Sparre-Andersen theorem with spatiotemporal correlations' (Phys. Rev. E 89:052111, 2014): physics-level, correlated random jumps, no theorem usable for deterministic drivers — skip. (3) Sparre Andersen universality itself does not transfer: exchangeability AND sign-invariance both provably fail for the drifted asymmetric +2/-1 counter; only Berger-Bethencourt's excursion/local-time technique is transferable, not the distribution-free g(n). (4) One-sided Gottschalk-Hedlund ('Birkhoff sums bounded on ONE side at a point implies coboundary' for minimal/uniquely ergodic systems): two targeted searches found no citable theorem — classical GH needs two-sided bounds; the only trace is inside 'Nonconventional results of Livsic and Gottschalk-Hedlund' (ResearchGate-gated). Treat as unproven folklore; do not cite without verification. (5) inria.hal.science full-text PDFs are blocked by the Anubis anti-bot wall — don't re-try that route for the Altman-Gaujal-Hordijk MOR paper; the van der Laan thesis (downloaded) contains the needed statements. (6) Gaubert-style full-language worst case is vacuous for Terras-bijective parity maps (every word occurs), so unconstrained (max,+) certificates cannot work; only language-constrained or orbit-conditioned versions can — consistent with our <=7-state DFA negative result, and now reinforced by Daviaud-Guillon-Merlet undecidability for the general weighted case.
